judge_details.reasoning: The dialogue lacks authentic workplace friction and reads more like mutual admiration than real coworkers. Characters sound too similar in register and enthusiasm, with no distinct voices — Margaret, Marcus, and Steph all speak in the same reflective, affirming cadence. The 'cold-brew angle' reference appears without setup and feels like a hallucination since cold coffee was mentioned offhandedly, not as a recipe technique.
strengths: Margaret's opening line ('Coffee's cold. We're doing jalapeño corn dog bites this week.') has a terse, distinct voice
The pivot from cold coffee to recipe technique has potential as a character beat
issues: All three characters who speak sound nearly identical in tone and vocabulary
No actual tension or disagreement — everyone just validates each other
Julian, Devon, and Steph are largely absent or interchangeable
The 'cold-brew angle changing cornbread structure' feels invented and unexplained
No specificity about the actual recipe — jalapeño heat level, batter ratios, mini muffin pan technique, nothing concrete
